executive_summary: Systemic risks are elevated. The high Supply Chain Stress score coupled with low global fuel reserves and a Middle East energy spike suggest potential cascading failures. The Taiwan Media Panic versus Physical Change disparity warrants monitoring as a potential leading indicator.
correlations: Supply Chain Stress and Global Fuel Reserves are inversely linked. A high Supply Chain Stress Score indicates difficulty in fuel distribution, exacerbating the impact of already low reserves.
